replies.author: @Speedster-bu6vf
replies.text:
The flaw in 'Woke' logic is the assumption that certain groups are inherently more 'moral' than others. It forgets that every human is capable of being horrible.

Historically, power and opportunity were concentrated in the largest groups—meaning they were simply the ones in the position to commit those acts. It’s not about identity; it’s about human nature and the circumstances of history.

The 'Woke' movement has traded individual accountability for group-based guilt. It assumes that if you belong to a certain racial or gender group, you are a villain, while ignoring that every single human is capable of being awful.

We have female presidents and CEOs who are just as bad at presidenting as their male counterparts. We have black people who are as racist or more racist than their white counterparts. And we have alphabet people who are as much or more intolerant than straight people.

Everyone of every race, every gender and every preference or identity is capable of being both awesome or horrible. That is what equality means, and I'm tired of pretending it is not.

replies.author: @candyice7465
replies.text: Point taken about peoples through the world and throughout history suffering unspeakable violence and enslavement. However, a correction about Anthony Johnson - NOT the first legal slave owner in the U.S. The first legally documented enslaved person in the U.S. (legally being a key word here) was John Punch, and Hugh Gwyn (and assigns) was the person to whom Punch was indentured when he was sentenced to lifelong servitude. What landed Punch in this situation was escape from indentured servitude, along with two men of European descent. When captured and tried, the men of European descent were required to add a few additional years to their indentured servitude while Punch was to serve for life, which set the stage for legal enslavement thereafter. So Hugh Gwyn became the first legally documented owner of an enslaved person. Anthony Johnson was the first to sue for lifelong ownership of a person years later. No reason why Hollywood shouldn't tell these tales, accurately, in all their nuance.

author: @MariaIsabellaZNN
text:
John Oliver is a truly, truly evil man. First time I saw a video of his was when someone sent it to me, because, I am European, and I lived in a "favored migration destination" during the big invasion of 2015, and they thought it would calm me down a little.
Oliver then had some kind of footage with a little girl, very polite, intelligent, in a wheelchair, speaking fluent English. After talking to her for a bit, he concluded with something along the lines "Well only a very mean and nasty person would not want that little girl in their country!"
However, I lived at the border, and I was a nurse, so I had helped out in one of those processing centers.. and those kind of little girls did not exist. It was 90% young men, sinister looking, and giving you glares of pure burning hatred for being an unveiled "non-believing" woman. The soldiers who were there as interpreters, and originated from where these people came from, were spat at and called as "colluding with the ENEMY" by them. They come here, and see this country as the enemy, and openly said so. There were also a few choice wives, all highly pregnant. Children were quite rare, but they definitely spoke not a lick of English.
I don't know if Oliver managed to cherry pick the little girl he showed or if she was just a stuido-hired plant, but the way he presented it, was a malicious lie any way you slice it. Sometimes I lie awake, horrified at knowing people watch this guy and believe his lies. He is an awful person, and if Hell is real, it exists for people such as him.

Well, yes, John, but YOU are the only person who has said that, and now your viewers will think that this is an actual argument put forward by the other side, when it isn't. Incredibly dishonest and manipulative. He plays it as if he really thinks he is 'on the right side of history' and wants to use his platform, but really he is just telling lies whilst pretending to tell jokes. He is evil, and rarely funny to boot.

replies.author: @Existential_Institute
replies.text:
@bassandtrebleclef Lol anytime someone disagrees with some thoughtless over-the--top reactionary critique they're a triggered snowflake. Breaking it down so the mouth breathers can comprehend: yes, John Oliver has parroted the woke party line over the years & produced some pretty cringe episodes, but lumping him in with every other brain-washed Hollywood actor is just ridiculous. He and his team produce thoughtful hilarious TV that often casts a light on issues where others don't want to look: insulin prices, medical debt, SLAPP suits, net neutrality, etc.

 I like Nerdrotic, but when he casually insults Oliver (and the more vapid section of his audience claps along like trained seals), he is not making a thoughtful, cutting critique of Hollywood -- he is revealing his own political biases. It's one thing to say that Hollywood is overrun with annoying woke sentiment. But when you argue that there should be no Trump critics left on TV? Why not just come out and say we should  censor the media and just have Rambo movies and Trump speeches on loop. THINK.
